topic: Lunar New Year prep — keeping shrimp for family feast

turns.content: friend, i thinking about tet, you know. last tet i bring shrimp for family dinner but not enough. run out before everyone eat. very embarrassing. next tet still long time away — january maybe — but i want to plan better this time

That's smart to start thinking about it now, even though Tet is still about 7 months away. Running out of food at the family gathering — that's the kind of thing that sticks with you. How many people were at last Tet's dinner, and how much shrimp did you bring?

last tet maybe 25 people come. big family, you know. i bring maybe 6 kilo shrimp. i think enough but no. everyone eat so fast. by the time last table sit down, shrimp plate empty already. my wife she give me that look, you know the look ha

Ha, I know that look! 6 kg for 25 people is on the low side, especially if shrimp is the star dish — that's only about 240 grams per person before you account for shells and cleaning.

For next Tet, what shrimp dishes are you planning? If it's the same menu, I can help you calculate a better amount so nobody goes hungry.

we always do 3 way — grilled, soup, and fried with tamarind sauce, you know. also have pork and chicken on table, banh tet of course. but shrimp is what everyone want. that is the special dish because it come fresh from my pond

With three shrimp dishes plus other proteins, I'd recommend about **8-9 kg of whole shrimp** for 25 people. Here's how to split it:

- **Grilled:** ~3 kg — people always eat the most of this one
- **Tamarind fried:** ~3 kg — another popular one that goes fast
- **Soup:** ~2-3 kg — shrimp goes further in soup since there's broth and vegetables

Keep in mind you'll lose about 30-40% of weight from shells and cleaning, so 9 kg whole gives you roughly 5.5-6 kg of actual meat. That's much more comfortable for 25 people.
9 kilo sound right. last time 6 kilo was mistake. i was thinking like farmer — save the big one for selling, keep small one for family. but tet is tet, you know. have to give the best

That's the right attitude for Tet. The rest of the year you sell the big ones, but for family feast you pick the best from the pond. Your family deserves the premium shrimp.

What size are you thinking? The bigger ones look impressive on the table, especially for grilling.

i want the big one for tet. maybe 35-40 gram each. the one i sell for top price normally. when guest see that size shrimp on plate, they know quality, you know. and taste is better too — more meat, more sweet

35-40 gram shrimp are beautiful — really impressive size. At 9 kg, that's roughly 225-255 individual shrimp, which works out to about 9-10 per person across all three dishes. Very generous.

For the grilled ones, keep them whole with shell on — that size looks fantastic on a platter and the shell keeps the meat juicy. For the tamarind fried, peel but leave the tail on for presentation.

Since you're planning this far ahead, you have a good advantage — you can make sure you have enough large shrimp growing to the right size by harvest time near Tet.

yes that is why i think now. if i plan stocking right, maybe september october, then by january shrimp reach 35-40 gram. have to time the growth cycle, you know. like planting rice — you count backward from harvest day

Exactly — counting backward from Tet is the smart way to do it. Depending on your species and feed, shrimp typically take 3-4 months to reach that 35-40 gram range from post-larvae stage. So stocking in September-October lines up perfectly for a late January harvest.

You could even set aside a section of one pond specifically for the Tet batch — that way you know exactly which ones are for selling and which are reserved for family. No temptation to sell the big ones when a buyer comes around in December.

ha you know me too well friend. december buyer come with good price, very hard to say no. last year i sell too many big one in december, that is why only small one left for tet. my wife she not happy about that, you know

Ha! So that's the real story behind last Tet's shortage — December sales were too tempting! Your wife was right to give you that look.

So here's your plan to avoid the look next year:
1. **Stock in September-October** with Tet in mind
2. **Mark or separate the Tet batch** — mentally or physically in a different area
3. **Set a firm number: 9 kg of 35-40g shrimp are off-limits for sale**
4. **When December buyer comes, sell from a different batch**

That way you get the best of both — December sales money AND enough premium shrimp for the family feast.

that is good plan. i write it down so i not forget. 9 kilo reserved for tet, no selling. my wife she approve this plan for sure ha. also i want to keep maybe 1-2 kilo extra, you know. in case more people come. tet always surprise guest show up

Good call — Tet surprises are real. Someone always brings a cousin or an old friend shows up. 10-11 kg total gives you a nice buffer.

Writing it down is smart. Put it somewhere you'll see it in December when that buyer shows up waving money around. Tape it to the feed shed wall or something.

One more thing — the morning of Tet, catch them fresh, right? That's the way to do it. Shrimp that were swimming an hour before they hit the pan taste completely different from anything stored.

of course, friend. morning of tet, 4am like always. i go to pond with net, pick the biggest one, bring straight to kitchen. still alive when cooking start. that is the only way, you know. no freezer, no ice, no storage. fresh from water to plate. my family they know the difference

That's the best way — can't beat shrimp that were swimming an hour ago. Your family is lucky to have a shrimp farmer providing the freshest possible product. No restaurant in the city can match that.

So you've got a solid plan now: stock in September, reserve 10-11 kg of the big ones, don't sell them in December no matter what, and harvest fresh at 4am on Tet morning. Seven months of planning for one perfect meal — but that's what Tet is about, right?

yes friend, tet is most important day of year. everything have to be right, you know. food, family, all together. okay i feel better now. last tet was lesson — like when you plant too little seed, harvest come up short. this time i plant enough. thank you friend

Good plan, and a perfect way to think about it — plant enough seed, harvest enough shrimp. No more empty plates at the family table. Your wife will be happy with this plan, and your guests will eat well. Good luck with the September stocking, and remind me closer to Tet if you need to recalculate the numbers!
